title: plagiobothrys tenellus var. echinatus
intro: Plagiobothrys tenellus var. echinatus, also known as California prickle poppy or prickly popcorn flower, is a variety of flowering plant in the borage family that is native to the western United States. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it useful for treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Plagiobothrys tenellus var. echinatus include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water to create a tea, or by making a tincture using the plant's root and stems.
